title: Living-world play relevance phase
summary: Lane 4 should now make living-world systems matter to play instead of reading as flavor only. This wave adds one route-aware world-state opportunity pass, then either deepens or trims the support-choice layer so it creates a felt difference in outings.

execution_order.why_now: Living-world state is ready to become soft gameplay, not just flavor.
One route should materially feel different because of weather, phenology, or process timing.
The new behavior should deepen replay and ecology, not add opaque gating.
Support choices should either matter to play or stay deliberately tiny.
The worst outcome is a loadout row that sounds flavorful but feels the same.
The support layer should create real difference without becoming inventory sprawl.

guardrails: No new shell, quest log, or inventory layer.
Prefer soft opportunity, clue emphasis, and replay value over hard gating.
If a support cannot create a felt difference, simplify instead of adding more support UI.

main_224_focus.copy_goal: turn the existing peak-phenology replay cue into a coherent active outing, not just a board-only note

recommended_variant.activeSummary: This is a good outing to finish the inland line while the short-season ground is clearest.

guardrails: Keep completion logic and inland route order unchanged.
Do not widen into support-row differentiation in this pass.
Keep the variant active-only instead of changing a filed identity.

main_225_focus.selected_prompt_text: What hints at a very short summer?
main_225_focus.copy_goal: keep place-tab distinct on the live tundra survey capstone instead of falling back to the same detail text as hand-lens
guardrails: Reuse the existing tundra ecosystem-note prompt instead of authoring a new support system.
Keep hand-lens, note-tabs, and route-marker behavior unchanged.
Make the distinction apply to both generic Tundra Survey and peak Bright Survey.
